location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: Base pay range $148,000-$185,000 in San Francisco, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, with significant time spent on consumer-facing or platform products
Track record of building and shipping 0-to-1 products with measurable user and business impact in ambiguous, fast-moving environments
Experience navigating complex cross-functional environments where you influence without direct authority
Strong business judgment, with the ability to balance end-consumer and partner needs
Data fluency - you define and analyze metrics that inform the success of your products, and you're comfortable running and interpreting quantitative analysis and experiments
Strong communication skills across audiences and levels, and experience working with external technical partners and senior leadership
Customer obsessed - strong user empathy and experience in the customer discovery and product development process
Familiarity with the operational complexity of physical-world services — where software launches intersect with on-the-ground readiness

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideally have experience with platform products, technical integrations, or partnership-heavy environments
Bonus: familiarity with autonomous vehicles, developer platforms, mobility, or marketplace dynamics

scope.surface: Lyft AV Platform — integrating and scaling AV technology partner rides on Lyft's network across cities
scope.business_model: B2C rideshare platform with B2B AV technology partnership layer
primary_metrics_inferred: AV ride quality and rider satisfaction
AV partnership scale (cities, rides, fleet size)
User and business impact of launched features
core_responsibilities: Own the product strategy to launch, scale, and deepen select AV partnerships in new cities, aligning your team's goals, success metrics, and roadmap to align with Lyft's mission and autonomous vehicle strategy
Serve as the primary product counterpart for AV technology partners, deeply understanding their capabilities and finding solutions that balance the needs of riders, partners, and Lyft
Drive a customer obsessed and learning culture by continuously shipping and iterating on products via user research, market research, data analysis, & experimentation
Be a valued collaborator for business development, strategic and other product partners - finding common ground that meets the needs of all parties

Lead a cross-functional pod of engineers, designers, data scientists, legal, policy, and business stakeholders - setting context, maintaining momentum, and building tools and processes to keep the team aligned
Collaborate across the company and communicate roadmaps, priorities, and decisions clearly to audiences ranging from partner teams to executive levels
Leverage AI tools and emerging technology to accelerate your team's workflows and enhance the products you build

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($148,000-$185,000 base in SF)
Equity mentioned as part of total package
Clearly defined product surface (AV Platform) with strategic importance called out
Hybrid schedule is specific (Mon/Wed/Thu), reducing ambiguity
Generous parental leave (18 weeks) and benefits package detailed
Flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Hybrid requires 3 days/week in San Francisco office — limits non-SF candidates
AV domain is fast-changing with partnership complexity; role scope may shift significantly with partner landscape

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD statement 'Salary ranges are dependent on... geographic location')
ambiguities: 'Familiarity with the operational complexity of physical-world services' appears in the Experience section without a 'preferred' or 'required' qualifier — classified as hard given placement in the main experience list, but could be soft.
The JD does not specify whether equity is in the form of RSUs or options.
